column10: Cell-to-cell signaling via diffusible autoinducers that coordinates group behaviors (bioluminescence, biofilm formation, virulence) when population density exceeds a threshold concentration.
column11: Alma et al show that there is a short interaction range for microenviroments (~ 20um). This work does not have 3D dimensional space. It is done in a 2D planar chip, confined to one cell thickness.

column7: Microcolonies → Microenvironment → Nutrient patches → Local communities -> Meta communities and meta populations
column8: Area in m^2 but depth might not be in meters.

column10: Metabolic exchange between microbial species where the waste product of one organism serves as a nutrient for another, creating obligate or facultative syntrophic interactions.
column11: Patches for bacterial cells Patch size: micrometers to centimeters, often submillimeter scale for dissolved organic matter patches. Patch lifetime: seconds to minutes for ephemeral patches, hours to days for persistent ones (e.g., around particles).

column6: 1.00E-03: 1 cubic cm to soil aggregate scale
column11: Physical conjugation/transduction scale. Evolutionary spread of transferred genes is global but conflates process with consequence.
